ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a chicken-and-egg English muffin breakfast burger with hashbrown. The broader 'sandwich food product' (FOODON_00002383) is used as the closest defensible anchor. A more specific term for breakfast burger set meals is absent from FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers composite sandwich/burger dishes. No exact code for chicken-egg English muffin breakfast burger with hashbrown exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024; broader composite dish code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this specific composite breakfast burger product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 280 g, comprising one assembled English muffin chicken-and-egg burger (approximately 210 g) plus one hashbrown patty (approximately 70 g), as commonly served in Singapore fast-food and school-canteen contexts.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken and egg breakfast burger with hashbrown — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Chicken and egg breakfast burger with hashbrown' (cr_id F08020010). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients not reported in the HPB record are set to -1 (data not available).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; English muffin and composite fast-food breakfast sandwich analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific composite product. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate carrier being an English muffin (GI ~77 per University of Sydney GI Database, wheat-flour English muffin) blended with a deep-fried hashbrown (GI ~70 for fried potato products) and protein/fat components that moderate the overall glycaemic response. Weighted composite estimate yields approximately GI 65. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (65 × 35.9 g available carbohydrate in 280 g serving) / 100 = 23.4. Documented as analogous estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 466 mg/100 g with low potassium offset (127 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: A standard 280 g serving delivers approximately 1305 mg sodium — exceeding 65% of the 2000 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals — and should be avoided or consumed only occasionally with no added condiments or sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (19.1 g/100 g) with medium GI (~65) and limited dietary fibre (2.13 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The medium GI and moderate carbohydrate load (approximately 53 g per serving) require careful portion control for individuals with type 2 diabetes; omitting the hashbrown reduces the glycaemic load by approximately 30% and is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (466 mg/100 g), deep-fried hashbrown contributing saturated and likely trans fats, and processed cheese and mayonnaise adding saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high sodium, deep-fried potato, processed cheese, and mayonnaise creates a high cardiovascular risk profile; individuals with established CVD or dyslipidaemia should limit consumption to occasional intake and request preparation without added salt or high-fat condiments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried hashbrown generating advanced glycation end products (AGEs) and pro-inflammatory fatty acid profile
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The deep-fried hashbrown generates AGEs that exacerbate joint inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis; patients should omit the hashbrown and request grilled rather than fried preparation where possible.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken and Egg Breakfast Burger with Hashbrown is a Western-style fast-food breakfast set meal widely available in Singapore's fast-food outlets, school canteens, and coffee shops. It consists of a seasoned and grilled or pan-fried chicken patty, a fried egg, a slice of processed cheese, and mayonnaise, all sandwiched between two toasted English muffin halves, served alongside a deep-fried hashbrown patty. The burger has a savoury, slightly creamy flavour profile from the mayonnaise and melted cheese, with a soft muffin exterior and a crispy hashbrown on the side. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 231 kcal, 11.85 g protein, 9.67 g fat, and 19.1 g carbohydrate, with 466 mg sodium — a level that is clinically significant for hypertensive individuals. The moderate protein content (11.85 g/100 g) supports satiety, but the high sodium, deep-fried hashbrown, and processed cheese make this a high-concern food for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 65 (medium), driven by the English muffin and hashbrown starch, with dietary fibre at 2.13 g/100 g providing limited glycaemic buffering. The meal is commonly consumed at breakfast or as a working lunch and is considered halal. It is not suitable for low-sodium, low-fat, or cardiovascular-protective diets without significant modification.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08020010) at the original confidence level of 0.95. The food is an exact match in the HPB record. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence component) but is documented with a defensible analogous-food basis from the University of Sydney GI Database. Overall record confidence is rated high given the exact HPB source match for all nutritional values.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite product is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F08020010) as of 2026. USDA FoodData Central does not contain an exact match for this Singapore-specific composite product. Fatty acid data remain unavailable from approved sources. 2. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo record for this item and no approved secondary source provides exact values for this composite product. 3. GI value of 65 is an analogous estimate based on English muffin (GI ~77, University of Sydney GI Database) and fried potato hashbrown (GI ~70) weighted by carbohydrate contribution and moderated by protein and fat content; this is not a directly measured value. 4. Serving size of 280 g is an estimate based on typical Singapore fast-food portion sizes for this category; no standardised gram weight is published in the HPB record. 5. The input record listed cholesterol as 11.99 mg/100 g — this is an unusually low value for a product containing egg and chicken; it is preserved as reported from the HPB source without modification. 6. No zero-macro justification required: all core macros (protein_g 11.85, fat_g 9.67, carbohydrate_g 19.1) are non-zero. 7. Q8 aliases self-check (iteration 3): all strings present in aliases_by_language values — english: ['Chicken and Egg Breakfast Burger with Hashbrown', 'Chicken and egg breakfast burger with hashbrown', 'Chicken egg burger with hashbrown', 'Breakfast chicken burger with egg and hashbrown', 'English muffin chicken egg burger'], chinese: ['鸡肉蛋早餐汉堡配薯饼', '雞肉蛋早餐漢堡配薯餅'], malay: ['Burger Sarapan Ayam dan Telur dengan Hashbrown'], tamil: ['சிக்கன் மற்றும் முட்டை காலை உணவு பர்கர் ஹாஷ்பிரவுனுடன்'], hindi: ['चिकन और अंडा ब्रेकफास्ट बर्गर हैशब्राउन के साथ'] — all 10 strings verified present verbatim in flat aliases array.
